summary: End-to-end product, governance, artifact, packaging, and installed-plugin validation for Agentic SDLC 0.5.0.

commands.summary: 82 tests passed after the complete hardening set, including exact delegated authorization scope, approval provenance, story/contract consistency, task-start freshness, checkpoint isolation, assessment routing, format enforcement, independent visual evidence, portable paths and IDs, Windows installer boundaries, concurrency, real tarball installation, and packaging.

commands.command: targeted product-governance, portability, installer, and supply-chain regression scenarios
commands.summary: Negative scenarios reject mismatched stories/contracts, bootstrap or revoked approvals, authorization subject/artifact drift, delegated install approval, self-referential visual evidence, Windows device IDs, junction destinations, and cross-platform path separators; workflow assertions accept both LF and Windows CRLF checkouts.
CLI and asset generator syntax checks passed.
Version, manifest entry point, skills, preset, and project KB checks passed.
Codex plugin manifest and package structure are valid.

commands.command: real npm tarball install and installed-package doctor
commands.summary: The test suite builds the actual tarball, installs it into a clean prefix, verifies required templates and assessment skill files, and runs the installed CLI doctor.

commands.command: release and CI workflow YAML parse
commands.summary: Release publication depends on the full Linux, macOS, and Windows matrix for Node 18.18, 20, and 24.

commands.command: clean personal-marketplace reinstall and installed CLI doctor
commands.summary: The enabled installed plugin reports 0.5.0 and its CLI SHA-256 matches the repository source.

commands.command: installed Codex Italian assessment checkpoint scenario
commands.summary: The plugin explained observed facts, architecture gaps, assumptions, unknowns, and exact approval scope, then stopped at Checkpoint 1 without producing an assessment.

commands.command: real DOCX and XLSX generation, render inspection, output linking, and strict gates
commands.summary: Both OOXML artifacts received passed verification receipts with visual evidence and zero strict-gate errors.

notes: The full installed Codex checkpoint transcript and rendered artifact smoke tests were executed in disposable temporary repositories; the durable pass summary is recorded here.
The first full matrix run exposed a Windows-only CRLF assumption in a release-workflow assertion; the assertion now validates both portable line-ending forms and the complete local suite passes.
The clean GitHub Actions Linux, macOS, and Windows matrix is required before this hardening commit is considered delivered.
Public GitHub release publication is intentionally not performed without an explicit version-tag release action.
